engineCodes.h2: Cadillac Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Cadillac engine — GM High Feature V6, EcoTec Turbo I4, and Northstar V8 families. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Cadillac specialists.

groups.name: GM High Feature V6 Family (3.6L)
groups.failureNote: The GM High Feature 3.6L V6 family (LY7/LLT/LFX) is known for timing chain tensioner failure and carbon buildup on intake valves. Our rebuilt units include upgraded tensioners, revised guide materials, and decarbonised ports to prevent repeat failures.

groups.name: EcoTec Turbo I4 Family (2.0L/2.7L)
groups.failureNote: EcoTec turbo I4 engines are known for turbocharger wastegate sticking and high-pressure fuel pump wear. Our rebuilt units include remanufactured turbo assemblies, updated fuel system components, and OEM-spec gaskets for long-term reliability.

groups.name: Twin-Turbo V6 & NA V6 Family (3.0L/3.6L)
groups.failureNote: Twin-turbo V6 engines are known for turbo actuator failure and intercooler condensation issues. Our rebuilt units include refurbished turbo actuators, upgraded intercooler seals, and comprehensive dyno testing to ensure performance matches factory specifications.

groups.name: Northstar V8 Family (4.6L)
groups.failureNote: Northstar V8 engines are infamous for head bolt thread failure in the aluminium block, leading to coolant leaks and overheating. Our rebuilt units include threaded head bolt inserts (Timesert®), updated gasket sets, and pressure-tested blocks to eliminate this classic failure mode.

groups.name: Small Block V8 Family (5.3L/6.0L/6.2L)
groups.failureNote: Small Block V8 engines with Active Fuel Management (AFM/DOD) are known for lifter collapse and camshaft wear. Our rebuilt units include deleted or upgraded lifters, revised camshafts, and optional AFM delete kits to prevent future failures while maintaining performance.

groups.name: Early Turbo & Entry Engines (2.4L/2.8L)
groups.failureNote: Early turbo V6 and entry-level I4 engines are known for turbocharger bearing wear and timing chain guide degradation. Our rebuilt units include remanufactured turbo cores, upgraded chain guides, and comprehensive balancing to restore original performance characteristics

commonProblems.h2: Common Cadillac Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: While Cadillac’s V6 and V8 families deliver strong performance, specific design compromises have created predictable failure patterns that dominate UK replacement demand. The 3.6 L V6, 2.0 L turbo, and 6.2 L V8 families account for the majority of workshop visits due to timing chain stretch, piston ring wear, and AFM valvetrain collapse. Every Cadillac engine rebuilt and supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components specifically designed to resolve these original weak points, and comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. The breakdown below is a practical decision tool: it maps real-world UK repair tiers against dealer and specialist pricing, so you can compare costs objectively and choose the route that protects your Cadillac engine without paying main-dealer premiums.

problems.h4: Timing Chain & Phaser Failure – Why Cadillac Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Cadillac CTS, SRX, ATS (2009–2019, LY7, LLT, LFX engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Early GM 3.6 L V6 phaser units lack robust anti-drainback valves, while tight packaging accelerates plastic chain-guide wear. Reduced oil pressure at idle stretches the chains, causing cold-start rattling and eventual cam/crank timing drift.
problems.recommendation: If you hear a distinct metallic rattle within three seconds of cold start, inspect the timing chain immediately. A full phaser and chain replacement is perfectly sound when caught before skip occurs. However, once diagnostic codes flag cam/crank correlation faults or metallic particles appear in the sump, partial repairs will rarely outlast a complete Cadillac engine replacement. We advise sourcing a pre-built unit with upgraded guides and factory-spec phasers.

problems.h4: High Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Failure – The 2.0 Turbo Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Cadillac ATS, CTS 2.0T (2013–2019, A20NFT / 2.0T engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Direct-injection piston rings in early A20NFT variants are prone to carbon clogging under high thermal stress. Combined with aggressive PCV routing, blow-by bypasses the rings, causing rapid oil consumption and turbo oil starvation.
problems.recommendation: Catching PCV failure early can delay ring degradation, but visible blue smoke under boost or sustained low oil warnings mean ring seating has already failed. Given the depth of disassembly required for a ring replacement, many UK specialists recommend a fully rebuilt 2.0 L Cadillac engine as the safest route. It removes cylinder scoring risk and restores factory compression in a single fitment.

problems.h4: AFM Lifter & Camshaft Collapse – The V8 Cylinder Deactivation Issue
problems.affectedModels: Cadillac Escalade, CTS-V, SRX AWD (2008–2018, L94, L92, L99 engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Active Fuel Management relies on oil-pressure-actuated lifters to deactivate cylinders under light load. Internal lifter springs fatigue and collapse, grinding camshaft lobes and sending metallic debris through the main oil gallery.
problems.recommendation: A faint lifter tick that escalates to a rhythmic metallic clatter signals cam lobe failure. Once debris contaminates the oil circuit, replacing lifters alone risks bearing damage within months. We strongly recommend a fully rebuilt 6.2 L Cadillac engine with AFM hardware and software permanently disabled. It eliminates the root cause and delivers consistent power without the risk of repeat valvetrain failure.

problems.affectedModels: Cadillac CTS, SRX, BLS (2005–2014, 3.6 L & 2.8 L V6 engine codes)
problems.rootCause: GM’s integrated water pump and thermostat housing use glass-reinforced nylon that becomes brittle under repeated thermal cycling. Hairline cracks at sealing surfaces cause rapid coolant loss, leading to sudden overheating, warped aluminium cylinder heads, and blown head gaskets.
problems.recommendation: Address any external coolant seepage from the front housing immediately. If your Cadillac engine overheated, triggered a temperature warning, or began misfiring under load, cylinder head distortion is highly probable. At that stage, repeated gasket replacements often fail within 12–18 months. A reconditioned Cadillac engine with upgraded cooling hardware provides a permanent, warranty-backed solution.

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Cadillac
finalCta.paragraph: Engine failures on these models rarely appear without warning, but waiting for symptoms to worsen turns a manageable repair into a costly write-off. EngineMarket cuts through main-dealer pricing by connecting you directly with vetted UK Cadillac specialists who stock rebuilt, reconditioned, and remanufactured units designed to outlast the original components. With nationwide fitting networks, transparent supply-and-fit pricing, and minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranties on every unit, you can compare real quotes instantly and return to the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Cadillac Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Cadillac model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Cadillac Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Cadillac engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Cadillac Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for Cadillac CTS or SRX owners seeking factory reliability without the factory price, with upgraded timing chain kits addressing the common 3.6L V6 failure point.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,800 – £5,500 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Cadillac Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as reinforced timing chain tensioners for the LY7/LLT/LFX family and Timesert® head bolt inserts for Northstar V8s – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Cadillac Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available – ideal for older DeVille or Seville models where a full rebuild may not be economical.

types.title: Cadillac Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for Escalade or CT5‑V owners wanting a zero‑mile engine with full warranty reassurance.

types.title: Cadillac Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older BLS or early CTS models where a full recon isn't economical but immediate mobility is needed.

types.title: Cadillac Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Cadillac specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty – ideal for owners without garage access or specialist tooling.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £600 – £1,500 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Cadillac engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Cadillac model today.

engineSizes.h2: Cadillac Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Cadillac engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Cadillac 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L diesel was exclusively fitted to the Cadillac BLS (2006–2009), a Saab-derived model built for European markets. This Fiat-sourced Z19DTH unit shares architecture with Vauxhall and Alfa Romeo applications, and while robust when maintained, it is prone to EGR clogging and DPF-related issues that can escalate to turbo or injector failure. Replacement demand is low but specialist-supplied reconditioned units remain available for BLS owners in the UK
items.cta: Get quotes for Cadillac 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Cadillac 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L turbo petrol family powers entry-level ATS, CTS, and newer XT4/XT5 models, using GM's high-output LTG/A20NFT architecture. These direct-injection engines deliver strong performance but are known for piston ring carbonisation under high thermal load, leading to elevated oil consumption and potential turbo oil starvation if PCV systems are neglected. Replacement demand is moderate, with rebuilt units featuring revised ring packs offering the most reliable long-term fix

items.title: Cadillac 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 2.5L LCV four-cylinder was offered as a base engine in early ATS models for the UK market. While less complex than turbocharged variants, this unit can experience timing chain tensioner wear and carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection. Failure rates are relatively low, but when replacement is required, reconditioned units with updated tensioners provide cost-effective reliability

items.title: Cadillac 2.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.8L turbocharged V6 (LP9) appeared in early CTS, SRX, and BLS models, offering a balance of power and refinement. However, the twin-scroll turbocharger and high-pressure fuel pump are common failure points, while carbon buildup on intake valves can cause misfires and reduced performance. When turbo failure occurs, many UK specialists recommend a full reconditioned engine to avoid repeat issues

items.title: Cadillac 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L LF1 V6 was fitted to CTS and SRX models as a mid-range petrol option between 2010 and 2015. This direct-injection unit is generally robust but can suffer from variable valve actuator wear and timing chain tensioner fatigue, particularly in high-mileage examples. Replacement demand is modest, with rebuilt units offering upgraded actuators and tensioners for extended service life

items.title: Cadillac 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L V6 family (LY7, LLT, LFX, LGX) is Cadillac's most widely used petrol engine in the UK, appearing in CTS, SRX, STS, ATS, and XT models. This displacement dominates replacement demand due to a well-documented timing chain and phaser failure pattern: plastic guide wear and weak phaser anti-drainback valves cause chain stretch, cold-start rattling, and eventual cam/crank correlation faults. Once metallic debris enters the oil circuit, partial repairs rarely succeed — making a rebuilt engine with reinforced metal guides and revised phasers the most cost-effective long-term solution

items.title: Cadillac 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The supercharged 4.4L LC3 V8 powered the high-performance STS-V, offering substantial power but complex forced-induction plumbing. Common issues include supercharger coupler wear, coolant leaks from the charge cooler, and oil consumption from valve stem seals. Due to low UK volumes, replacement units are specialist-sourced and typically include refreshed supercharger components and upgraded cooling

items.title: Cadillac 4.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The Northstar 4.6L V8 (L37/LD8) featured in Seville, STS, and DTS models before 2011. Early versions (pre-2004) are notorious for head bolt thread failure in the aluminium block, while later iterations improved reliability but can still suffer from coolant leaks and oil consumption. Replacement demand is driven by catastrophic head gasket failure, with reconditioned units featuring thread-repaired blocks and updated gaskets

items.title: Cadillac 5.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.3L V8 (LMG/L59/L33) appears in imported Escalade models and shares architecture with GM truck platforms. Active Fuel Management (AFM) lifter collapse is the dominant failure pattern, often leading to camshaft lobe damage and metallic debris circulation. When AFM-related symptoms appear, many UK specialists recommend a rebuilt engine with hardware AFM delete to prevent recurrence

items.title: Cadillac 6.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.0L V8 (LQ4/L76) and its hybrid variant (LFA) powered early Escalade imports. Non-hybrid units share the AFM lifter vulnerability of smaller GM V8s, while the LFA hybrid system adds complexity with electric motor coupling and battery management. Replacement demand is low but specialist, with rebuilt units typically featuring AFM delete and refreshed hybrid components where applicable

items.title: Cadillac 6.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.2L V8 family (L92/L94/L86/LSA) powers high-spec Escalade and performance CTS-V/CT6-V models. Like other GM V8s with AFM, lifter collapse and camshaft wear are common failure points, while supercharged LSA variants (CTS-V) add supercharger coupler and coolant system vulnerabilities. Given the high value of these vehicles, rebuilt engines with AFM hardware delete and upgraded valvetrain components represent the most reliable replacement route

items.title: Cadillac 6.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 6.0L LFA hybrid system was offered in a limited number of Escalade Hybrid imports, combining a V8 with two electric motors for improved fuel economy. Replacement is highly specialist due to the integrated motor-generator units and high-voltage battery pack; failures typically involve battery degradation or inverter issues rather than the internal combustion engine itself. System / motor code(s): LFA (2-Mode Hybrid)
items.cta: Enquire about Cadillac 6.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Cadillac Electric Motors
items.description: The Cadillac Lyriq represents the brand's first dedicated BEV for the UK market, using GM's Ultium platform with permanent-magnet synchronous motors. Electric motor failure is rare, but high-voltage battery pack degradation or inverter faults can require specialist diagnosis and replacement. Due to the nascent UK presence, replacement units are sourced directly from GM-certified channels. System / motor code(s): Ultium Drive (front/rear motor variants)
items.cta: Enquire about Cadillac electric motor repair

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Cadillac needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Cadillac specialists.

fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Cadillac engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand‑wide issues, and UK specialist supply – from the most popular diesel workhorses to the latest electrified powertrains.
fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Cadillac has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options – all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Cadillac specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Cadillac Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Cadillac engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Cadillac specialists.

families.name: GM High Feature & LF-Series V6 Family (2004–2023)

entries.title: LY7 3.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The LY7 powers early UK CTS (2004–2007), SRX (2004–2009), and STS saloons. Premature timing chain tensioner wear and intake valve carbon buildup frequently cause cold-start rattles and misfires. Quality rebuilt units include uprated hydraulic tensioners, reinforced plastic guides, and professionally decarbonised cylinder heads.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LY7 Cadillac engine replacement

entries.title: LLT 3.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Found in CTS (2008–2014) and SRX (2010–2015), this direct-injection variant suffers from oil pump drive chain stretch and upper timing guide failure. Rebuilt replacements feature revised oil pump assemblies, updated chain guides, and factory-spec tensioner upgrades to prevent repeat timing jumps.
entries.cta: Compare LLT 3.6 Cadillac engine prices

entries.title: LFX 3.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Used extensively in ATS (2013–2019), CTS (2014–2019), SRX (2013–2016) and early XT5 models. Persistent PCV system failures and oil consumption lead to low-pressure warning lights and accelerated timing wear. Our network’s rebuilt LFX engines include updated PCV valves, reinforced piston rings, and revised timing sets.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt LFX Cadillac engines

entries.title: LGX 3.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The modern naturally aspirated V6 for CT6 (2016–2020), XT5 (2017–2023) and XT6 (2020–2025). While improved over predecessors, it still experiences timing chain stretch and occasional AFM-related lifter wear at high mileage. Rebuilt LGX units are fitted with reinforced timing chains, upgraded lifters, and precision-balanced crankshafts.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LGX Cadillac V6 replacement

entries.title: LFW 3.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Available in CTS (2008–2013) and first-generation SRX (2008–2015) models. Coolant crossover leaks and timing guide degradation commonly trigger overheating and catastrophic top-end damage. Specialist rebuilds include reinforced coolant passages, updated timing chain kits, and pressure-tested cylinder blocks.
entries.cta: Compare LFW 3.0 Cadillac engine costs

entries.title: LH2 4.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Powering the DTS (2006–2011) and late DeVille saloons, this transverse-mounted V8 is infamous for aluminium block head bolt thread failure under thermal cycling. Rebuilt Northstar engines incorporate factory-approved Timesert® thread inserts, upgraded multi-layer steel head gaskets, and revised oil cooler seals.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LH2 Northstar Cadillac engine

entries.title: LD8 4.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Fitted to Cadillac DeVille (1998–2005) and base Seville models (1998–2004). Identical thread-stripping failure points to the LH2, compounded by early-generation water pump impeller cracking. Our rebuilt LD8 units feature precision-threaded inserts, remanufactured water pumps, and factory-spec valvetrain components.
entries.cta: Compare LD8 Cadillac V8 replacement prices

entries.title: L37 4.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The high-output Northstar variant found in Seville STS (1998–2004) and Eldorado ETC (1998–2002). Higher compression increases head gasket stress and accelerates valvetrain wear if timing is slightly off. Rebuilt L37 engines include reinforced timing gears, uprated valve springs, and fully pressure-tested blocks.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt L37 Northstar Cadillac engines

families.name: GM Small Block & LT-Series V8 Family (2002–2025)

entries.title: LH6 5.3L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Used in early Escalade (2002–2006) models. Active Fuel Management (AFM) was not yet fitted, but piston ring wear and plastic timing guide degradation cause heavy oil consumption. Rebuilt LH6 units feature upgraded piston rings, reinforced chain guides, and revised valve cover gaskets.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LH6 Cadillac V8 replacement

entries.title: LMG 5.3L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Common in Escalade (2007–2014) and imported SUV trims. AFM lifter collapse and subsequent camshaft lobe destruction represent the primary catastrophic failure point. Specialist rebuilds include AFM delete hardware, hardened lifters, remanufactured camshafts, and upgraded valley covers.
entries.cta: Compare LMG 5.3 Cadillac engine prices

entries.title: L86 6.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Fitted to Escalade (2015–2020) and high-spec CT5 models. Dynamic fuel management and high displacement increase lifter stress, with AFM collapse remaining the leading cause of total engine failure. Rebuilt L86 engines are supplied with reinforced camshafts, uprated lifters, and revised oil pump assemblies.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt L86 Cadillac Escalade engines

entries.title: L87 6.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The current-generation Small Block for Escalade (2021–2025). Despite improved oiling, DFM actuator failures and turbo/oil line cracking on performance variants drive early replacements. Rebuilt L87 units include DFM delete components, reinforced oil lines, and dyno-tested main bearings.
entries.cta: Get quotes for L87 6.2 Cadillac V8 replacement

entries.title: LSA 6.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The supercharged heart of CTS-V (2009–2015) models. Supercharger coupler wear, intercooler seal degradation, and AFM lifter failure under boost are common high-mileage issues. Performance rebuilds feature reinforced supercharger snouts, upgraded main bearings, and AFM elimination kits.
entries.cta: Compare LSA supercharged Cadillac engine costs

entries.title: LT4 6.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Powers the CT5-V Blackwing (2022–present) and track-focused models. Extreme cylinder pressures accelerate crankshaft bearing wear and turbocharger shaft failure. Rebuilt LT4 engines include forged rotating assemblies, precision-balanced crankshafts, and upgraded main bearing caps.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt LT4 Cadillac performance engines

families.name: EcoTec Turbo I4 & Compact V6 Family (2006–2025)

entries.title: LE5 2.4L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Used exclusively in the UK-market BLS (2006–2009). Timing chain guide fragmentation and balance shaft bearing wear lead to severe top-end noise and eventual seizure. Rebuilt LE5 units are fitted with reinforced chain kits, updated balance shaft bearings, and revised oil pan gaskets.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LE5 Cadillac BLS engine

entries.title: LP9 2.8L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The rare turbocharged V6 in first-generation SRX (2006–2009). Turbo bearing failure under UK stop-start conditions and intercooler condensation damage are the primary failure drivers. Rebuilt LP9 engines include remanufactured turbo cores, upgraded intercooler hoses, and reinforced exhaust manifolds.
entries.cta: Compare LP9 2.8T Cadillac engine prices

entries.title: LTG 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Powers ATS (2013–2019), CTS (2014–2019), XT4 and early XT5 models. High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) internal wear and turbo wastegate sticking cause lean misfires and loss of boost. Rebuilt LTG units feature revised HPFP plungers, remanufactured turbo actuators, and updated timing tensioners.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt LTG 2.0T Cadillac engines

entries.title: LSY 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The updated EcoTec turbo in CT4 (2020–present), CT5, XT4 and XT6. Turbo oil feed line cracking and HPFP seal degradation are common failure points. Specialist rebuilds include braided stainless turbo feed lines, upgraded HPFP seals, and reinforced timing assemblies.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LSY 2.0T Cadillac replacement

entries.title: L3B 2.7L Petrol
entries.description: Engine The high-output turbo four in CT4 and CT5 (2020–present). Carbon accumulation on intake valves and turbo actuator electrical failures drive early replacement requests. Rebuilt L3B engines include walnut-blast decarbonised ports, refurbished actuator assemblies, and reinforced cam followers.
entries.cta: Compare L3B 2.7T Cadillac engine costs

entries.title: Z19DTH 1.9L Diesel
entries.description: Engine The sole diesel option for the UK BLS (2006–2009), sourced from the Fiat/GM partnership. Injector nozzle failure, EGR valve carbon blockage, and DPF regeneration issues commonly lead to complete engine shutdown. Rebuilt Z19DTH units include refurbished injectors, EGR bypass/upgrade options, and new turbochargers. Closing line (Block A): Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full Cadillac engine code directory below — every code from the last 30 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

engineYears.h2: Cadillac Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers Cadillac's complete UK and global engine timeline from 1997 to the present across every major model line - from the Northstar V8 era through the LD8/L37 families, the GM High Feature V6, the LT-series Gen V V8s, and the Blackwing supercharged performance pinnacle - so owners can identify exactly what was fitted to their vehicle by year and compare rebuilt Cadillac engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Northstar 4.6 V8 Era Establishes & Catera Diesel Arrives Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The second-generation Northstar 4.6 V8 was firmly established as Cadillac's signature engine across all flagship models, while the Catera brought GM's European V6 to the range for the first time.
keyChanges: Northstar 4.6 LD8 (275 hp) and L37 (300 hp) variants dominant across DeVille, Eldorado, Seville, and STS
Cadillac Catera launched with GM/Opel 3.0 V6 (54-degree design) - shared with Omega/Commodore
Northstar aluminium block with front-wheel-drive transverse mounting standard on DeVille and Eldorado
Head bolt thread stripping in aluminium block identified as an emerging problem on early 1993–1996 units - affects 1997 cars with early production tolerances
Euro 2/3 compliance in force on European-market Cadillacs
mainEngines: Northstar LD8 - 4.6 naturally aspirated V8 275 hp (DeVille, Eldorado base, Seville SLS)
Northstar L37 - 4.6 naturally aspirated V8 300 hp (Seville STS, Eldorado ETC)
GM 3.0 V6 (54-degree) - 3.0 petrol V6 (Catera)
knownFor: Northstar 4.6 delivers exceptional refinement and performance for its era - smooth, high-revving V8 with genuine 150 mph capability in STS specification; widely considered one of the most advanced American engines of the 1990s

Head gasket failure due to aluminium block head bolt thread stripping is the Northstar's defining long-term weakness - torque-to-yield bolts stretch under sustained heat loading and threads pull from the soft aluminium block, typically presenting between 80,000–130,000 miles
Northstar coolant system requires Dex-Cool long-life coolant at precisely maintained intervals - neglected coolant is the primary trigger for overheating events that initiate bolt failure
Catera 3.0 V6 considered underpowered and reliability mixed by US owner forums - timing belt failure a known issue; not a highly regarded Cadillac engine by specialists
years.cta: Find rebuilt 1997–2000 Cadillac Northstar 4.6 engine prices from UK and US specialists

years.preview: Escalade V8 Expansion, SRX Introduction & Northstar Continues Performance Landmark
years.description: The Escalade's arrival as a genuine luxury SUV powerhouse expanded Cadillac's V8 range to include GM's truck-derived LS-family engines alongside the car-based Northstar, while the SRX and CTS brought new architectures to the brand.
keyChanges: Cadillac Escalade (2001) offered with 5.3 LM7 V8 and 6.0 LQ9 V8 - GM LS-generation truck architecture
CTS first generation launched (2002–2003) with 3.2 V6 (LA3) and 5.7 V8 (LS6) performance option
SRX launched (2004) with 3.6 V6 and 4.6 Northstar V8
Northstar 4.6 continues across DeVille DTS, Seville STS - revised valve cover sealing attempts to address oil leaks
Head bolt revised specification TSB issued for Northstar 1995–2005 production range
mainEngines: Northstar LD8/L37 - 4.6 V8 (DeVille DTS, Seville STS, SRX 4.6)
GM LQ9 - 6.0 V8 truck-derived (Escalade 6.0)
GM LM7 - 5.3 V8 (Escalade 5.3)
GM LS6 - 5.7 V8 (CTS-V first generation - 400 hp)
LA3 - 3.2 V6 (CTS base)
knownFor: LS6 V8 in first CTS-V is a landmark performance engine - 400 hp, pushrod V8, shared with Corvette Z06; robust, specialist-grade architecture that easily exceeds 150,000 miles
GM truck V8 family (LM7/LQ9) in Escalade considered highly durable - conventional pushrod architecture, well-proven in commercial applications

Northstar 4.6 head bolt issue peaks during this period as 1997–2000 fleet ages - overheating, coolant loss, and full engine replacement are the most common Northstar-era search queries from this generation
CTS 3.2 V6 (LA3) competent but underpowered relative to European segment rivals - valve cover and oil pan gasket leaks reported at higher mileages
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Cadillac Northstar 4.6 engine replacement - Cadillac DeVille engine head gasket failure - Cadillac Escalade 6.0 engine replacement - Cadillac CTS-V LS6 engine rebuild
years.cta: Compare 2001–2004 Cadillac engine prices - Northstar, LS6, and Escalade V8 specialists

years.preview: Final Northstar Generation, STS-V Supercharged & New 3.6 V6 Performance Landmark
years.description: The fourth-generation Northstar received its final significant update, the supercharged LC3 variant delivered 469 hp in the STS-V and XLR-V, and the new High Feature 3.6 V6 arrived as the architecture that would define Cadillac's non-V8 lineup for the next decade.
keyChanges: Northstar LC3 4.4 supercharged V8 introduced in STS-V and XLR-V (2006) - 469 hp, Eaton TVS supercharger 🔴 Performance Landmark
High Feature LY7 3.6 V6 introduced (2004–2005) - DOHC, 255 hp; replaces 3.2 LA3 in CTS and SRX
Final generation Northstar 4.6 (2005 update) - revised head bolt specification, Dex-Cool system update
CTS gains STS-based 3.6 V6 and 6.0 V8 options
DTS replaces DeVille as flagship front-wheel-drive saloon - Northstar LD8/L37 carried over
mainEngines: Northstar L37/LD8 - 4.6 V8 (DTS, STS base)
Northstar LC3 - 4.4 supercharged V8 469 hp (STS-V, XLR-V)
High Feature LY7 - 3.6 DOHC V6 (CTS, SRX, STS)
LH2 - 4.6 V8 Northstar (STS 4.6, SRX 4.6)
knownFor: LC3 supercharged Northstar in STS-V and XLR-V is a genuine performance landmark - 469 hp with Cadillac's characteristic smoothness; however the supercharged unit inherits all of the base Northstar's head bolt vulnerabilities and a blown supercharger nose-piece seal is an additional failure category

Final Northstar 4.6 remains a refined and capable engine when properly maintained - TSB-revised head bolt specification from 2005 offers modest improvement but does not fully resolve the thread-in-aluminium-block architecture limitation

High Feature 3.6 V6 (LY7) introduces a new reliability baseline - DOHC design is smooth and capable but timing chain wear on early units (from 60,000 miles) and valve cover gasket oil leaks are the most cited issues by US and UK specialists
Northstar replacement engine demand peaks strongly during 2005–2010 as the large 1997–2003 fleet ages into the critical mileage window
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Cadillac Northstar engine replacement 2006 - Cadillac STS 4.6 engine failure - Cadillac DTS engine replacement - Cadillac 3.6 V6 timing chain replacement - Cadillac STS-V supercharged engine rebuild
years.cta: Get 2005–2007 Cadillac Northstar and 3.6 V6 engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

years.preview: CTS Reborn, LF1/LFX 3.6 V6 & Escalade Gen IV Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The second-generation CTS launched as Cadillac's most credible global executive car, paired with the revised 3.6 direct-injection LF1/LFX V6 family that would power the brand for the next decade, while the fourth-generation Escalade delivered the 6.2 L92/L9H V8.
keyChanges: CTS second generation (X322) launched (2008) - 3.0 LFW and 3.6 LF1 direct-injection V6; first globally competitive Cadillac saloon
Northstar discontinued (2011) - final use in DTS and STS; replaced by LFX across future sedans 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction (LFX replaces Northstar)
CTS-V second generation launched (2009) with LS9 6.2 supercharged V8 - 556 hp, shared with Corvette ZR1 🔴 Performance Landmark
Escalade Gen IV (2007 launch, dominant from 2008) with 6.2 L92 V8 and 6.0 LFA variant
DI (Direct Injection) arrives across 3.6 V6 family - LF1, later updated to LFX
mainEngines: LF1 / LFX - 3.0/3.6 direct-injection V6 (CTS, SRX second gen, Escalade entry)
LFW - 3.0 V6 direct-injection (CTS base)
LS9 - 6.2 supercharged V8 556 hp (CTS-V second generation)
L92 - 6.2 V8 (Escalade, Escalade ESV/EXT)
LFA - 6.0 V8 (Escalade hybrid)
knownFor: LS9 supercharged V8 in second-gen CTS-V is among the finest performance engines ever fitted to a production saloon - 556 hp, zero-lift intake design, forged internals; exceptionally durable with correct oil service intervals; a specialist-grade heritage unit

LF1/LFX 3.6 direct-injection V6 is a significant improvement over LY7 - however carbon build-up on intake valves (a characteristic of direct-injection without port injection) and timing chain wear from 80,000 miles remain the two most common US and UK specialist complaints

Final Northstar DTS/STS generation generates heavy replacement demand between 2010–2016 as those vehicles age - considered by specialists as one of the most commercially significant Cadillac engine replacement categories
Second-generation SRX 3.6 V6 suffers from timing chain and water pump failure at moderate mileages - widely cited on SRX owner forums; replacement demand significant in the used SUV market
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Cadillac CTS 3.6 engine replacement - Cadillac SRX 3.6 timing chain failure - Cadillac CTS-V LS9 engine rebuild - Cadillac Escalade 6.2 engine replacement - Cadillac DTS Northstar replacement engine
years.cta: Source 2008–2012 Cadillac CTS, SRX, and Escalade rebuilt engine quotes today

years.preview: ATS, ELR Hybrid & LTG 2.0 Turbo Introduction Major Engine Family Introduction Technology Introduction
years.description: Cadillac's smallest model, the ATS compact saloon, brought the new LTG 2.0 turbocharged four-cylinder into the range alongside the 2.5 LSY and 3.6 LFX, while the ELR plug-in hybrid coupe marked Cadillac's first electrified powertrain.
keyChanges: ATS (X300) launched (2013) with LTG 2.0T, 2.5 LSY, and 3.6 LFX - Cadillac's first genuinely compact rear-wheel-drive platform
LTG 2.0 turbocharged petrol four-cylinder introduced - 272 hp; first turbocharged four-cylinder in modern Cadillac range 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
ELR (2014) - Volt-derived plug-in hybrid with 1.4 range-extender petrol + electric motor; Cadillac's first PHEV 🔴 Technology Introduction
CTS third generation (X322 II) launched (2014) with LFX 3.6, LTG 2.0T, and TT 3.6 twin-turbo V6 (LF3/LF4)
XTS flagship launched (2013) with LFX 3.6 V6 as standard powertrain
mainEngines: LTG - 2.0 turbocharged petrol four-cylinder (ATS 2.0T, CTS 2.0T, XTS performance)
LSY / LCV - 2.5/2.0 naturally aspirated petrol four-cylinder (ATS 2.5 base)
LFX - 3.6 V6 direct-injection (CTS 3.6, ATS 3.6, XTS, SRX)
LF3 - 3.6 twin-turbo V6 420 hp (CTS-V Sport, CTS 3.6TT)
LF4 - 3.6 twin-turbo V6 640 hp (CTS-V third generation, from 2016)
ELR PHEV - 1.4 range-extender + Voltec electric drive (ELR)
knownFor: LTG 2.0 turbo is refined and competent - timing chain wear and oil consumption on early units reported from 60,000 miles; direct-injection carbon build-up on intake valves the same concern as the wider GM DI family

LF3/LF4 twin-turbo V6 family represents Cadillac's finest V6 output - 420 hp in LF3, 640 hp in LF4 CTS-V; considered highly reliable under correct service intervals by US specialists; the LF4 is a standout performance engine capable of very high mileages with premium oil
LFX continues its known timing chain and water pump vulnerability profile - third-generation SRX and XTS owners reporting failures from 70,000–90,000 miles
ELR range-extender hybrid is technically interesting but commercially unsuccessful - replacement battery demand is an emerging specialist area as these vehicles age
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Cadillac ATS 2.0 turbo engine replacement - Cadillac CTS 3.6 engine replacement 2014 - Cadillac LTG engine timing chain - Cadillac XTS 3.6 engine rebuild - Cadillac SRX engine replacement 2013
years.cta: Get 2013–2015 Cadillac ATS, CTS, and XTS engine replacement quotes from specialists

years.preview: CT6, LGW Twin-Turbo V6, LT4 Supercharged V8 & XT5 Performance Landmark
years.description: The CT6 flagship expanded the Cadillac engine palette with the exclusive LGW 3.0 twin-turbo V6, the CTS-V hit its apex with the LT4 supercharged 640 hp V8, and the new XT5 crossover replaced the SRX as the brand's core volume model.
keyChanges: CT6 launched (2016) with 2.0T LTG, 3.0TT LGW, and 3.6 LGX - flagship saloon with lightweight aluminium structure
LGW 3.0 twin-turbo V6 introduced exclusively in CT6 - 404 hp; bespoke engine not shared with other GM models
LT4 6.2 supercharged V8 640 hp in third-generation CTS-V (2016) 🔴 Performance Landmark
XT5 replaces SRX (2017) with 3.6 LGX V6 - revised DI + port injection (D-PVPI) system added to address carbon build-up
LGX 3.6 V6 updates LFX architecture with D-PVPI dual-injection - significant reliability improvement for intake valve carbon deposits
mainEngines: LGW - 3.0 twin-turbo V6 404 hp (CT6 3.0TT)
LGX - 3.6 V6 with D-PVPI dual injection (XT5, CT6 3.6, ATS, CTS)
LTG - 2.0 turbocharged petrol four-cylinder (CT6 2.0T, ATS, CTS)
LT4 - 6.2 supercharged V8 640 hp (CTS-V third generation)
L86 / LV3 - 6.2/4.3 V8/V6 (Escalade continued)
knownFor: LT4 supercharged 6.2 V8 in third-generation CTS-V is the definitive Cadillac performance engine of the modern era - 640 hp, positive-displacement supercharger, derived from Corvette Z06; widely regarded by US specialists as highly durable when properly maintained; runs conservatively on stock tune

LGW 3.0 twin-turbo V6 praised for smoothness and performance in CT6 - relatively rare unit means independent specialist expertise is limited in the UK; replacement parts availability is the primary long-term concern

LGX addresses the key criticism of LFX - dual-injection system substantially reduces intake valve carbon deposits; most LGX owners on high-mileage use report notably cleaner intake ports versus earlier DI-only units

CT6 lightweight aluminium structure and multi-material construction create a technically complex ownership proposition for independent UK specialists - engine maintenance straightforward, but surrounding systems require specialist-grade knowledge
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Cadillac CTS-V LT4 engine replacement - Cadillac XT5 3.6 engine replacement - Cadillac CT6 3.0 twin-turbo engine issues - Cadillac 3.6 LGX timing chain UK - Cadillac Escalade 6.2 engine rebuild
years.cta: Compare 2016–2019 Cadillac CTS-V, CT6, and XT5 engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: CT5, CT4, Blackwing Apex & Lyriq EV Transition Performance Landmark Technology Introduction
years.description: Cadillac's CT-series sedans replaced CTS and ATS, the CT5-V Blackwing delivered the ultimate LT4 668 hp supercharged V8 as the brand's combustion performance pinnacle, and the Lyriq signalled the full-electric future.
keyChanges: CT5 (2020) replaces CTS - 2.0T LTG and 3.0TT LGX-derived twin-turbo
CT4 (2020) replaces ATS - 2.0T LTG and 2.7T LSY-derived turbo four-cylinder
CT5-V Blackwing (2022) - LT4 6.2 supercharged V8 668 hp; highest output Cadillac road engine ever produced 🔴 Performance Landmark
CT4-V Blackwing (2022) - 3.6 twin-turbo LF4-derived V6 472 hp
Cadillac Lyriq BEV revealed (2021) - Ultium platform electric drive; Cadillac's first dedicated BEV 🔴 Technology Introduction
Escalade fifth generation (2021) with 6.2 L87 V8 and 3.0 inline-six diesel LM2
mainEngines: LT4 - 6.2 supercharged V8 668 hp (CT5-V Blackwing)
LF4-derived twin-turbo - 3.6 twin-turbo V6 472 hp (CT4-V Blackwing)
2.0T LTG / turbo four-cylinder - 2.0 petrol turbo (CT5 base, CT4 base)
2.7T LSY - 2.7 turbocharged four-cylinder (CT4, CT5)
L87 - 6.2 naturally aspirated V8 420 hp (Escalade fifth gen)
LM2 - 3.0 inline-six diesel (Escalade diesel - US market)
Ultium Electric Drive - dual/tri-motor BEV (Lyriq)
knownFor: CT5-V Blackwing LT4 is the unanimously acclaimed apex of Cadillac's combustion performance legacy - 668 hp, 6-speed manual available, 200 mph top speed; US and international specialist community rates it among the greatest performance saloons ever built

2.7T LSY four-cylinder in CT5/CT4 base models introduces a new turbocharged four-cylinder to the mainstream Cadillac range - early units show some oil consumption concerns; still too new for definitive high-mileage assessment
Escalade fifth generation L87 6.2 V8 is a mature, proven architecture - Air Ride suspension failures are the primary independent specialist complaint, not the engine itself

Lyriq BEV marks the official beginning of Cadillac's EV-first era - but the large global CT5, CT4, Escalade, and XT-series fleet ensures combustion engine replacement demand will remain strong well into the 2030s
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Cadillac CT5 engine replacement 2021 - Cadillac CT5-V Blackwing LT4 engine rebuild - Cadillac Escalade 6.2 engine replacement 2021 - Cadillac CT4 2.0 turbo engine issues - Cadillac XT5 3.6 engine replacement 2020
years.cta: Get 2020–2022 Cadillac CT5, CT4, and Escalade replacement engine quotes today

years.preview: Ultium EV Expansion, Final Combustion V8 Applications & Celestiq
years.description: Cadillac's Ultium-based EV range expands with Lyriq, Escalade IQ, and Celestiq, but the combustion V8 and turbo families continue in Escalade and CT5-V Blackwing - ensuring strong ongoing UK and global replacement demand.
keyChanges: Escalade IQ (2024) - full BEV Escalade on Ultium platform; up to 680 hp dual motor
Celestiq handbuilt EV flagship (2024) - Ultium platform, ultra-premium positioning
CT5-V Blackwing continues with LT4 6.2 supercharged V8 - no successor announced
XT-series (XT4, XT5, XT6) continue with LTG 2.0T and LGX 3.6 V6 on existing architectures
Increasing aftermarket demand as 2013–2018 ATS, CTS, and SRX fleet ages into 80,000–120,000-mile replacement window
mainEngines: LT4 - 6.2 supercharged V8 668 hp (CT5-V Blackwing - continuing)
LGX - 3.6 V6 dual-injection (XT5, XT6, CT5 - continuing)
LTG / LSY - 2.0/2.7 turbocharged four-cylinder (CT4, CT5, XT4)
L87 - 6.2 V8 (Escalade fifth gen - continuing)
Ultium Electric Drive - BEV (Lyriq, Escalade IQ, Celestiq)
knownFor: LT4 Blackwing V8 now achieves legacy status - demand for rebuilt and remanufactured LT4 units growing as first CT5-V Blackwing examples approach 40,000–60,000-mile territory
LGX 3.6 V6 timing chain wear on 2013–2019 vehicles continues to drive the largest volume of Cadillac engine replacement enquiries - XT5, CTS, and ATS owners most affected

Ultium-platform EVs bring new specialist challenges - battery management, thermal system maintenance, and high-voltage inverter service will define the next generation of Cadillac independent workshop expertise

First-generation LTG 2.0T ATS and CTS cars (2013–2018) entering peak replacement demand window - rebuilt LTG and LFX engines are the most commercially active Cadillac engine replacement categories for UK and US specialists currently

faq.h2: Cadillac Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Cadillac owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Cadillac engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Cadillac 3.6L V6 (LY7/LLT/LFX) typically costs £2,400–£3,600 from a specialist, while a 6.2L V8 for Escalade runs £2,800–£4,200; fitting adds £600–£1,200 depending on model complexity. Used units start around £800–£1,500 but carry higher risk without warranty. For context, main dealers often quote £5,000+ for the same work. Always confirm whether VAT, delivery, and fitting are included before committing.

items.question: What affects the cost of a Cadillac engine replacement?
items.answer: Four factors drive pricing: engine displacement (2.0T vs 6.2L V8), condition (used vs reconditioned), vehicle complexity (CTS vs Escalade), and service type (supply‑only vs supply‑and‑fit). Location matters too—London and Southeast fitting rates run 20–30% higher than regional specialists. Our comparison tool surfaces transparent, like‑for‑like quotes so you can weigh options without hidden surprises.

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Cadillac?
items.answer: In most cases, yes—especially if the chassis, gearbox, and interior remain in good condition. A rebuilt Cadillac engine with upgraded components (e.g., reinforced timing guides on the 3.6L V6) often outlasts the original unit and costs far less than purchasing a replacement vehicle. If your CTS or SRX holds £8,000–£15,000 value, engine replacement is almost always the smarter financial choice.
items.cta: Get a Cadillac engine quote now

items.question: What's included in a Cadillac engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: Supply‑and‑fit covers sourcing the correct engine code (e.g., LFX 3.6L), removing the failed unit, installing the replacement with new gaskets/fluids, disposing of the old engine, and a post‑fit test‑drive. Most specialists include a workmanship warranty alongside the engine warranty. Expect fitting to add £600–£1,200 depending on model access and regional labour rates.
items.cta: Get a Cadillac engine quote now

items.question: How long does a Cadillac engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like the CTS or SRX with the 3.6L V6, expect 3–5 working days from engine arrival to handover. More complex installs—Escalade V8s or CTS‑V supercharged units—may require 5–7 days. The biggest variable is engine availability; our network stocks commonly replaced codes (LFX, LSY, L94) to minimise wait times. Always ask suppliers about current lead times before booking.
items.cta: Get a Cadillac engine quote now

items.question: Do reconditioned Cadillac engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes—every reconditioned Cadillac engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Many specialists offer extended 24‑month coverage on premium rebuilt units. Warranty typically covers major internals: crankshaft, pistons, timing components, and oil pump. Always confirm exact terms, exclusions, and claim process directly with your chosen supplier before purchase.

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Cadillac engine?
items.answer: Used engines (£800–£1,500) suit tight budgets or short‑term ownership, but carry unknown mileage and limited warranty. Reconditioned units (£2,400–£4,200) include new bearings, seals, and upgraded parts addressing known failures—like metal timing guides for the 3.6L V6—and should deliver 100k+ miles with proper maintenance. If your Cadillac has a failure‑prone engine family, reconditioned is the safer long‑term investment.
items.cta: Compare used and reconditioned Cadillac engine options

items.question: Where can I find Cadillac engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of vetted Cadillac specialists, with strong coverage in the Midlands, Northwest, and Southeast. Our registration lookup matches your exact engine code (e.g., LY7, LFX, L94) to nearby suppliers who stock compatible units and offer fitting. Nationwide delivery is available if no local specialist holds your required engine.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Cadillac engine specialists

items.question: What are the most common Cadillac engine failures?
items.answer: The 3.6L V6 family (LY7/LLT/LFX) suffers timing chain and phaser failure; the 2.0T turbo (A20NFT/LSY) experiences piston ring carbonisation and oil consumption; AFM‑equipped V8s (L94/L86) face lifter collapse and camshaft wear; early 3.6L units had plastic thermostat housing cracks causing overheating. These failures often progress to catastrophic damage, making full engine replacement the most common outcome rather than repair.
items.cta: Get a Cadillac engine quote now

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Cadillac engine?
items.answer: Compare at least three quotes—prices can vary by £1,000+ on the same engine code. Consider used for tight budgets or imminent resale; choose reconditioned with upgraded parts for long‑term ownership. Ask whether quotes include fitting, delivery, VAT, and warranty registration. Supply‑only options can save £300–£600 if you have a trusted local garage willing to fit.
